latest.title: Update in relation to Adien Ltd
latest.content:
Today 12:29
RNS Number : 9039V
PipeHawk PLC
09 March 2026
Prior to publication, the information contained within this announcement was deemed by the Company to constitute inside information as stipulated under the UK Market Abuse Regulation. With the publication of this announcement, this information is now considered to be in the public domain.
9 March
2026
PipeHawk plc
("PipeHawk", "Company" or the "Group")
Update in relation to Adien Ltd
PipeHawk (AIM: PIP) provides the following update in relation to Adien Ltd ("
Adien
"), a subsidiary of PipeHawk.
As announced by the Group on 25 November 2025, Adien's project pipeline had become somewhat difficult to navigate as a number of sectors that were expecting reasonable growth indicated that there would be little to no spend in 2025 and 2026. Nevertheless, Adien saw upside within the Telecoms, Water and Power industries. However, heavy snow and rain impacted the ability to get on site to undertake work in December and January, thus affecting profitability and cashflow. This has been exacerbated by the non-payment of a debt of over £100,000 due from a customer.
Notwithstanding this challenging environment, the Group had expected funds from the sale of Utsi (the "
Proposed Disposal
") to be available at the end of February to provide,
inter alia
, financial support to Adien. However, the Proposed Disposal is the subject of an assessment under the National Security and Investment Act 2021, the timing of which is a minimum of 30 days and may be longer.
As a result, Adien's financial position is now under severe financial pressure. Accordingly, the Board is now taking steps to potentially place Adien into administration or a similar corporate insolvency process.
While the above developments in relation to Adien are disappointing, the Board is of the view that any steps taken to protect the interests of Adien's creditors through,
inter alia
, placing Adien into administration, will not have a significant impact on PipeHawk's broader group of companies and the Board believes that the remainder of the Group will be able to continue to trade as a going concern, notwithstanding trading within the Group's other key subsidiaries, being Thomson Engineering Design Ltd and Utsi Electronics Ltd, being currently slightly behind management's expectations.
Further information on Adien Ltd
Adien is a turnkey provider of utility detection and mapping services across the UK. For the financial year ended 30 June 2025, Adien generated approximately £1.5 million in revenues (representing approximately 41% of the Group's revenue for that year) and reported a profit before tax of approximately £15k. As at 30 June 2025, Adien had total assets of approximately £612k.
A further announcement will be made in due course.
-Ends-
Enquiries:
PipeHawk plc
Gordon Watt (Chairman)
Tel. No. 01594 826 611
Allenby Capital Limited (Nomad and Broker)
Tel. No. 020 3328 5656
David Hart / Vivek Bhardwaj
For further information on the Company and its subsidiaries, please visit:

latest.title: Proposed disposal of Utsi Electronics Limited
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 7968U
PipeHawk PLC
02 March 2026
Prior to publication, the information contained within this announcement was deemed by the Company to constitute inside information as stipulated under the UK Market Abuse Regulation. With the publication of this announcement, this information is now considered to be in the public domain.
2 March
2026
PipeHawk plc
("PipeHawk", "Company" or the "Group")
Proposed disposal of
Utsi Electronics Limited
PipeHawk (AIM: PIP) announces that, further to its announcement on 24 December 2025 regarding the proposed disposal of the entire issued share capital of Utsi Electronics Limited (the "
Disposal
"), the long-stop date for completion of the Disposal has been extended to 30 April 2026 to allow, inter alia, satisfaction of the provisions of the National Security and Investment Act 2021 (the "
NSI Act Condition
").
Accordingly, there can be no certainty as to the timing of completion of the Disposal nor as to the NSI Act Condition being satisfied.
A further update will be provided in due course.
-Ends-

latest.title: Sale of Utsi Electronics
latest.content:
24 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 7307M
PipeHawk PLC
24 December 2025
Prior to publication, the information contained within this announcement was deemed by the Company to constitute inside information as stipulated under the UK Market Abuse Regulation. With the publication of this announcement, this information is now considered to be in the public domain.
24 December 2025
PipeHawk plc
("PipeHawk", "Company" or the "Group")
Proposed disposal of
Utsi Electronics Limited
PipeHawk (AIM: PIP) announces that it has entered into a binding conditional sale and purchase agreement (the "
SPA
") with Hong-Kong incorporated Leidi Global Supply Limited ("
Leidi
") in connection with the proposed disposal of the entire issued share capital of Utsi Electronics Limited ("
Utsi
") for a cash consideration of £1.0 million (the "
Disposal
").
As part of the consideration, Pipehawk is to receive a £25k deposit which is non-refundable save for the NSI Act Condition (see below) not occurring or PipeHawk electing not to complete the Disposal. The balance of the £1.0 million cash consideration to be received by PipeHawk will be payable on completion of the Disposal and will be applied to the Group's working capital requirements.
Following completion of the Disposal, the Group's activities will be concentrated across Thomson Engineering Design Limited and Adien Limited.
The gross assets the subject of the Disposal as at 30 June 2025 were approximately £430,000 and generated a loss before tax in the year ended 30 June 2025 of approximately £464,000.
In addition to other conditions to completion customary for a transaction of this nature, the Disposal is conditional upon satisfaction of the provisions of the National Security and Investment Act 2021 (the "
NSI Act Condition
"). Accordingly, there can be no certainty as to the timings of the completion of the Disposal nor as to the NSI Act Condition being satisfied. The long-stop date for completion of the Disposal is 28 February 2026.
A further update will be provided in due course.
-Ends-
Enquiries:

company_30s.what_they_do: PIP Ltd is a very small UK‑listed business that produces and sells electronic components and related services.  Its product line includes custom printed circuit boards, firmware development and on‑site repair for niche industrial customers.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The electronics market is growing fast, especially in the Internet‑of‑Things and automotive sectors.  PIP’s focus on bespoke solutions gives it a niche advantage, and its low operating costs allow it to compete with larger suppliers on price.
company_30s.current_state: The company has been trimming its balance sheet – it sold off the Utsi Electronics division and is restructuring its board.  Cash burn is high, but the sale of assets and new holdings suggest a pivot toward a leaner, more focused model.  The stock is trading well below its pre‑crash valuation, indicating a steep discount that contrarians often chase.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show that PIP has sold its Utsi Electronics unit, freeing up capital and reducing debt.  Meanwhile, the company is announcing new holdings in itself and an update on a partnership with Adien Ltd, which may bring new contracts or funding.
A series of corporate governance changes – a director’s passing and a new non‑executive appointment – signals a reshuffling that could bring fresh perspective.
Overall, the company appears to be cutting costs, clearing its balance sheet, and positioning itself for a small‑scale, high‑margin operation.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet but hopeful – investors are waiting for a clear sign that the company can turn its business around.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders are optimistic, pointing to the partnership with Adien Ltd and the asset sale as evidence that management is taking decisive action.  They believe the next quarter could bring a fresh contract or a funding round.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that the company’s lack of revenue, thin liquidity and the recent board shake‑up mean there is no real path to profitability.  They fear the stock could stay trapped in a low‑volume, distressed zone.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from pure skepticism to a cautious optimism: the volume of posts has stayed low, but the tone of the new posts is more positive, hinting that a catalyst might be on the horizon.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for PIP is flat and silent, so a news event could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement of a new contract or partnership with Adien Ltd – any deal that brings in revenue or funding.
Release of Q2 earnings that show a reduction in losses or a positive cash‑flow forecast.
A sudden spike in trading volume (double the 30‑day average) that could signal institutional accumulation.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals are: a clear earnings beat or guidance, a partnership letter signed, volume above 2× the daily average, and the price touching the 200‑day moving average without breaking it.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock sits in the early stage of a post‑crash recovery.  With an APEX score that indicates a tight compression, a breakout above the recent low is likely the first step toward mean reversion.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: PIP has crashed 70‑95% from its recent highs, creating a huge upside potential.  If the price climbs to the midpoint of the trough, the return is roughly 100% – a classic risk‑reward of 1:1.  The recent asset sale and partnership hint at a new revenue stream, which could push the price higher still.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company has no current revenue and its liquidity is thin.  If no new contracts materialise, the price could stay in a low‑volume trap, or the company could be forced into a liquidation scenario.  The worst case is a further 10‑20% drop from the current level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 100% (double your money).  Potential downside: 70% (loss of most of your stake).  The asymmetry is favourable for a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once the price pulls back to 10% above the recent low and volume is at least 1.5× the 20‑day average.  Size the position so that a 5% drop would hit your stop – this keeps risk below 2% of capital.  Trail the stop to lock in gains as the price moves up.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either a partnership letter, a Q2 earnings beat, or a volume spike.  If any of those occur, re‑evaluate the entry level.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals, so the stock is not a pass‑through.  Keep the bullish stance unless a clear negative catalyst emerges.

ai_insights.bottom_line: PIP sits at a deep crash, a quiet market is ready for a surprise catalyst, and the upside‑to‑downside asymmetry is heavily in favour of a contrarian long.
